name: Sierra de Santa Lucía Trail
name_original: Castillo de Luna – running circular desde Alburquerque
description: Jog the moderate 3.7-mile Sierra de Santa Lucía Trail near Alburquerque, featuring 368 feet of elevation gain and views of Castle of Luna.
region_intro: Jogging around Alburquerque offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by its varied terrain, including trails through natural areas and around local landmarks. These routes provide opportunities to experience the local environment. Best jogging routes around Alburquerque The most popular jogging route is Sierra de Santa Lucía Trail, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route features gentle elevation changes suitable for a consistent pace. Another top favourite among local runners is Casa Comandante loop from Cuartel de Menacho Bótoa, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain with minimal elevation gain, ideal for a steady run. Local runners also love the Running loop from Cuartel de Menacho Bótoa, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail leading through local surroundings, often completed in about 44 minutes. Jogging around Alburquerque is defined by its accessible paths and local scenery. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate trails. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Alburquerque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alburquerque?
A: There are over 15 jogging routes available in Alburquerque, with more than 200 runners having explored the area using komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences for different fitness levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of jogging routes in Alburquerque?
A: The jogging routes in Alburquerque primarily range from easy to moderate. There are 4 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, providing options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Alburquerque?
A: Yes, Alburquerque offers several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31571939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Escuela Infantil Kidsco Botoa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 km long and takes approximately 30 minutes to complete with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I find moderate running trails in Alburquerque?
A: Absolutely. The majority of running trails in Alburquerque are moderate. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992976340/sierra-de-santa-lucia-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Santa Lucía Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.9 km route with gentle elevation changes, typically completed in about 41 minutes.

Q: What is a good option for a longer running route in Alburquerque?
A: For a slightly longer run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31578289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Comandante loop from Cuartel de Menacho Bótoa&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route spans 7.3 km and offers a mix of terrain with minimal elevation gain, ideal for a steady, extended run.

Q: What interesting sights can I see near the jogging routes in Alburquerque?
A: While jogging in Alburquerque, you can explore several interesting sights. These include historical landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4429266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Campo Maior&lt;/a&gt;. For a unique experience, you might also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4429272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capela dos Ossos de Campo Maior&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Are there any natural features like waterfalls near Alburquerque's running trails?
A: Yes, for those who enjoy natural scenery, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6545309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabroeira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction located near Alburquerque, offering a refreshing sight to complement your run.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Alburquerque?
A: Many of Alburquerque's running paths are designed as loops, offering convenient circular routes. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31578289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Comandante loop from Cuartel de Menacho Bótoa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31576305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cuartel de Menacho Bótoa&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alburquerque?
A: Runners often praise Alburquerque for its accessible paths and varied local scenery. The region offers a good mix of terrain, from gentle trails to those with slight elevation changes, making it enjoyable for different running preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Alburquerque?
A: Alburquerque generally offers a favorable climate for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal conditions for each trail vary, the region's overall environment is conducive to running for much of the year. It's always advisable to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are the running routes in Alburquerque suitable for families?
A: Many of the easy to moderate routes in Alburquerque, particularly those with minimal elevation, can be suitable for families looking to enjoy a run together. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31571939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Escuela Infantil Kidsco Botoa&lt;/a&gt; are generally less challenging and can be a good option for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails in Alburquerque?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail, many outdoor areas in Alburquerque are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain a pleasant environment for all users.
tips:
Luna Castle, also known as Alburquerque Castle, is an impressive medieval fortress located in the town of Alburquerque, in the province of Badajoz, on top of a hill in the Sierra de San Pedro, dominating the town of Alburquerque and offering panoramic views of the Los Baldíos region. 
Built between the 13th and 15th centuries, the castle was expanded and reinforced by Álvaro de Luna, Master of the Order of Santiago and Constable of Castile, hence its name. 
Luna Castle is one of the most outstanding castles in Extremadura and a must-see for lovers of history and medieval architecture.
